---
title: "House Price Prediction"
date: 2026-02-04
categories: [Python, Machine Learning, Pricing]
image: "https://imagescdn.homes.com/i2/SKVfiu2zkYAVlBV4bA7WVLhlkFAf8HXy-LGAqGlBc78/116/northridge-heights-ames-ia.jpg"
description: Predicting house prices using property features from the Ames Housing dataset.
---

![](https://imagescdn.homes.com/i2/SKVfiu2zkYAVlBV4bA7WVLhlkFAf8HXy-LGAqGlBc78/116/northridge-heights-ames-ia.jpg?p=1){fig-align="center" width=100%}

*Northridge Heights, Ames, Iowa — source: [homes.com](https://imagescdn.homes.com/i2/SKVfiu2zkYAVlBV4bA7WVLhlkFAf8HXy-LGAqGlBc78/116/northridge-heights-ames-ia.jpg?p=1)*

## Problem
Predict house prices based on the characteristics of residential properties.

## Data
The **Ames Housing Dataset** contains detailed information on residential property sales in Ames, Iowa, USA, covering the period **2006–2010**.  
The dataset includes **79 property features**, such as size, location, quality of construction, and other housing characteristics.

## Method
Exploratory data analysis was performed to understand the dataset and identify relationships between property features and sale prices.  
Feature selection was based on correlation analysis, followed by feature engineering to create stronger predictors.  
A **Ridge regression model** was then used to predict house prices.

## Tools
Python, pandas, NumPy, matplotlib, Ridge regression

## Result
Built a predictive model that explains 92.5% of the variability in house prices.

## Full Analysis
[Open the full notebook](../notebooks/house_price_prediction.html)
